title: [5] MCP Server Phase 4 — Live Tunnel Validation
why_we_need_this: Phases 1-3 (tasks #2-#4, all completed) produced the tool-surface design, then shipped read-only tools (Phase 2) and mutating CRUD tools with confirmation gates (Phase 3) — all built, vetted, and unit-tested in isolation only, never exercised against a live dashboard over a real WireGuard tunnel (`project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` L78). Per the owner-approved 5-phase roadmap, Phase 4 is the validation gate before Phase 5 packaging: every MCP tool must be proven to work against the actual production dashboard over the actual tunnel, not mocks, before this is considered usable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Read `project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` in full (current STATUS, all documented tools including the now-resolved `get_health` exception, the confirmation-gate mechanics for edit/enable/disable/add vs. the token-gated delete dry-run flow) plus `mcp/docs/tool-surface.md` and any `mcp/docs/confirmation-gates.md` (Phase 3 deliverable) to get the full list of tools requiring validation. Cross-check `clients-connectivity/README.md` via `search_project_context` for the invariants this phase must validate against (SQLite as live source of truth, parity with the `wg-peer` CLI, admin_peer seed rule).
- With the operator's laptop connected to this project's WireGuard tunnel (not this project's other/unrelated VPN servers), exercise every shipped tool against the real dashboard at its configured address (default `172.16.15.1:8080`, per Phase 2's env/CLI override mechanism) — not a local/mocked dashboard instance.
- Validate all read-only tools (including `get_health`) return data consistent with what the live dashboard UI shows for the same peer/metrics/service state at the same moment.
- Validate all `confirm=true`-gated mutating tools (`add_client`, `edit_client`, `enable_client`, `disable_client`): confirm the tool refuses to mutate when `confirm` is missing/false, and correctly applies the mutation via live `wg-sync` (no tunnel drop, no instance replacement) when `confirm=true`, matching `clients-connectivity/README.md`'s "live apply" invariant.
- Validate the `delete_client` token-gated dry-run flow end-to-end against the live dashboard: `preview_delete_client` returns accurate current peer state and a token; `delete_client` succeeds with a valid unexpired token bound to the correct peer name; `delete_client` correctly refuses with a missing, wrong, or expired token (test the documented expiry window from Phase 3, e.g. by waiting past it or using a stale token).
- Confirm no tool ever touches SQLite or `wg` directly — all validated behavior must be attributable to the dashboard's `/api/*` HTTP responses (wrapper-only invariant).
- Confirm the MCP server behaves correctly as a stdio-spawned subprocess in this live setting (starts on demand via an MCP host config, exits cleanly, no lingering process, no Docker, no always-on listener).
- Use a non-critical/test peer (not a peer relied on for real access) for all add/edit/enable/disable/delete validation — do not mutate the `admin_peer` or any peer the operator depends on for tunnel access during this task.
- Document the validation results (pass/fail per tool, any discrepancies found) in `mcp/docs/` (e.g. append to `tool-surface.md` or a new `mcp/docs/phase4-validation.md`) so Phase 5 and future readers have a record without re-running the exercise.
- If any tool fails live validation, document the failure and its root cause in the same file — do not silently fix and re-test without recording what broke; if a fix is out of this task's scope, flag it back rather than expanding scope silently.
important_constraints:
- This is Phase 4 of the approved 5-phase roadmap — validation only. Do not add new tools, do not change the confirmation-gate design (RESOLVED in Phase 3: `confirm=true` for edit/enable/disable/add, token dry-run for delete only) — re-litigating that design is out of scope.
- Do not modify `dashboard/` or `terraform/` — this task only validates existing behavior and adds a results doc under `mcp/docs/`.
- Do not mutate the `admin_peer` or any peer the operator relies on for real tunnel access — use a disposable test peer for all mutating-tool validation.
- No application-layer auth is being added or discussed here — the no-auth-at-the-dashboard-boundary model remains a settled, owner-accepted risk; this task validates existing confirmation gates, not auth.
- Do not proceed to Phase 5 (packaging/host config wiring) as part of this task — that is separate scope, queued only after this task's results are reviewed.
- Do not edit anything under `project-context/` directly — that's Praxis-managed via `update_project_context`, out of scope for this task.
- Follow this repo's git conventions once results/docs are committed: PR-based workflow, `docs:` commit prefix (or `test:` if that fits the repo's existing prefix set better — check recent commit history), `docs/`-prefixed branch name.
- After this task's work lands, run `update_project_context` so the mcp-server route's STATUS line reflects Phase 4 as complete (or documents specific failures if validation surfaced problems) instead of "not started."
